HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

SommerfeldIntegrator Class Reference
Modules » Green's Functions

Numerical engine for Sommerfeld integrals in stratified media. More...

Private Attributes

std::vector< dcmplx > k_L
 Wavevector of the layered medium.
 
int materialIndex
 Observation layer index (and source for intra-layer)
 
int sourceIndex
 Source layer index.
 
std::vector< dcmplx > epsilon
 Permittivity of the layered medium.
 
std::vector< dcmplx > mu
 Permeability of the layered medium.
 
std::vector< double > zValsInterfaces
 Interface z-values.
 
std::vector< double > thickness
 Layer thicknesses.
 
double semiReal
 Scaling factor for real axis of semiellipse.
 
double semiImag
 Scaling factor for imaginary axis of semiellipse.
 
double ratio
 z:r ratio
 
std::map< std::string, double > options
 Options for ODE integration.
 
double cutoff
 Cutoff parameter.
 
LayeredMediaUtils * layeredUtils
 Layered media utility functions.
 

Detailed Description

Numerical engine for Sommerfeld integrals in stratified media.

Construct with material stacks and numerical knobs (semi-ellipse radii, ratio, cutoff), then use Evaluate() to get intra-/inter-layer Green coefficients packed for interpolation.

Definition at line 36 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ SommerfeldIntegrator() [1/2]

SommerfeldIntegrator::SommerfeldIntegrator ( const std::vector< dcmplx > k_L,
int inMaterialIndex,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inEpsilonMedium,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inMuMedium,
const std::vector< double > & inZValsInterfaces,
const std::vector< double > & inThickness,
double inSemiReal = 4.0,
double inSemiImag = 0.05,
double inRatio = 1.0,
double inCutoff = 0.1 )

Constructs a SommerfeldIntegrator object for layered media calculations.

This constructor initializes the Sommerfeld integrator with the given parameters, which define the properties of the layered medium and numerical settings for integration.

Parameters
k_LA vector of complex wave numbers for the layered medium.
inMaterialIndexAn integer indicating the observation/source intra-layer index for calculations.
inEpsilonMediumA vector of complex permittivity values for the medium layers.
inMuMediumA vector of complex permeability values for the medium layers.
inZValsInterfacesA vector of interface positions along the z-axis.
inThicknessA vector of layer thicknesses.
inSemiRealScaling factor for real axis of semiellipse.
inSemiImagScaling factor for imaginary axis of semiellipse.
inRatioA double defining the integration ratio parameter.
inCutoffA double specifying the cutoff value for the integration.

Definition at line 511 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

SommerfeldIntegrator::SommerfeldIntegrator ( const std::vector< dcmplx > k_L,
int inMaterialIndex,
int inSourceIndex,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inEpsilonMedium,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inMuMedium,
const std::vector< double > & inZValsInterfaces,
const std::vector< double > & inThickness,
double inSemiReal = 4.0,
double inSemiImag = 0.05,
double inRatio = 0.8,
double inCutoff = 0.1 )

Constructs a SommerfeldIntegrator object for layered media calculations.

This constructor initializes the Sommerfeld integrator with the given parameters, which define the properties of the layered medium and numerical settings for integration.

Parameters
k_LA vector of complex wave numbers for the layered medium.
inMaterialIndexAn integer indicating the observation layer index for calculations.
inSourceIndexAn integer indicating the source layer index for calculations.
inEpsilonMediumA vector of complex permittivity values for the medium layers.
inMuMediumA vector of complex permeability values for the medium layers.
inZValsInterfacesA vector of interface positions along the z-axis.
inThicknessA vector of layer thicknesses.
inSemiRealScaling factor for real axis of semiellipse.
inSemiImagScaling factor for imaginary axis of semiellipse.
inRatioA double defining the integration ratio parameter.
inCutoffA double specifying the cutoff value for the integration.

Member Function Documentation

◆ Evaluate() [1/2]

CoeffsInter SommerfeldIntegrator::Evaluate ( const dcmplx & tq_te,
const dcmplx & tq_tm,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & r,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & z1,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & z2,
bool singular,
const std::string & op = "" )

Computes inter-layer coefficients for Green's function interpolation.

Parameters
tq_teQuasistatic TE transmission coefficient.
tq_tmQuasistatic TM transmission coefficient.
rA 3D vector representing radial coordinate values.
z1A 3D vector representing axial coordinate values for the first layer.
z2A 3D vector representing axial coordinate values for the second layer.
singularA boolean indicating whether singularity subtraction is required (always true).
opA string specifying additional operations (always empty).
Returns
A CoeffsInter structure containing Green's functions interpolation coefficients.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid singular value is provided.

Definition at line 1663 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ Evaluate() [2/2]

CoeffsIntra SommerfeldIntegrator::Evaluate ( const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_te,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_tm,
const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & r,
const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & z,
bool singular,
const std::string & op = "" )

Computes intra-layer coefficients for Green's function interpolation.

Parameters
rq_teQuasistatic TE reflection coefficients.
rq_tmQuasistatic TM reflection coefficients.
rA 2D vector representing radial coordinate values.
zA 2D vector representing axial coordinate values.
singularA boolean indicating whether singularity subtraction is required (always true).
opA string specifying additional operations ('plus', 'minus', or empty for default behavior).
Returns
A CoeffsIntra structure containing Green's functions interpolation coefficients.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid singular value is provided.

Definition at line 1445 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ FresnelCoeff()

std::vector< dcmplx > SommerfeldIntegrator::FresnelCoeff ( int layerIndexI,
int layerIndexJ,
dcmplx krho,
const std::string & waves ) const

Computes the Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients between two layers.

This function calculates the Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients for TE (transverse electric) or TM (transverse magnetic) waves at the interface between two material layers.

Parameters
layerIndexIIndex of the first layer.
layerIndexJIndex of the second layer.
krhoThe transverse wave vector component.
wavesA string specifying the wave type ('TE' or 'TM').
Returns
A vector containing:
  • The Fresnel reflection coefficient.
  • The Fresnel transmission coefficient.
Exceptions
std::out_of_rangeif the layer indices are invalid.
std::invalid_argumentif the wave type is not 'TE' or 'TM'.

Definition at line 1940 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ integrationFInter()

IntegrandInter SommerfeldIntegrator::integrationFInter ( const dcmplx & tq_te,
const dcmplx & tq_tm,
const Data3D & inData,
double x,
double k_max,
const IntegrandInter & intZeroKrho,
const std::string & path )

Computes the inter-layer integration function.

This function evaluates the integral of the inter-layer Green's function over a specified path. The integration can be performed along a semi-ellipse, real infinity, or imaginary infinity.

Parameters
tq_teQuasistatic TE transmission coefficient.
tq_tmQuasistatic TM transmission coefficient.
inDataA Data3D structure containing spatial coordinate information.
xDouble representing the integration parameter.
k_maxParameter for integration path specification.
intZeroKrhoAn IntegrandInter structure containing precomputed singularity corrections.
pathA string specifying the integration path ('semi', 'real', or 'imag').
Returns
An IntegrandInter structure containing the integrated Green function elements.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid path type is specified.

Definition at line 1279 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ integrationFIntra()

IntegrandIntra SommerfeldIntegrator::integrationFIntra ( const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_te,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_tm,
const Data2D & inData,
double x,
double k_max,
const IntegrandIntra & intZeroKrho,
const std::string & path,
const std::string & op = "" )

Computes the intra-layer integration function.

This function evaluates the integral of the intra-layer Green's function over a specified path. The integration can be performed along a semi-ellipse, real infinity, or imaginary infinity.

Parameters
rq_teQuasistatic TE reflection coefficients.
rq_tmQuasistatic TM reflection coefficients.
inDataA Data2D structure containing spatial coordinate information.
xDouble representing the integration parameter.
k_maxParameter for integration path specification.
intZeroKrhoAn IntegrandIntra structure containing precomputed singularity corrections.
pathA string specifying the integration path ('semi', 'real', or 'imag').
opA string specifying additional operations ('plus', 'minus', or empty for default behavior).
Returns
An IntegrandIntra structure containing the integrated Green function elements.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid path type is specified.

◆ interDist()

std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > SommerfeldIntegrator::interDist ( std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > z,
int index,
double direction )

Computes distances from interfaces for inter-layer secondary wave calculations.

This function calculates the distances of given points from an interface of a layer, adjusting the calculations based on the specified index and direction.

Parameters
zA 3D vector representing the z-coordinates of points.
indexThe index of the layer of interest.
directionA double indicating the direction of propagation.
Returns
A 3D vector containing the computed distances from the interface.

Definition at line 688 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ InterIntegrand()

IntegrandInter SommerfeldIntegrator::InterIntegrand ( const dcmplx & tq_te,
const dcmplx & tq_tm,
const Data3D & inData3D,
dcmplx kr,
const std::string & mode )

Computes the inter-layer Green's function integrand.

This function evaluates the inter-layer integrand for a given wavenumber, using radial and axial coordinates. It computes Green function elements, derivatives, and surface integrals based on mode selection and operations.

Parameters
tq_teQuasistatic TE transmission coefficient.
tq_tmQuasistatic TM transmission coefficient.
inData3DData3D structure containing spatial coordinate information.
krComplex radial wavenumber.
modeString specifying the mode of operation ('bessel' or 'hankel').
Returns
An IntegrandInter structure containing computed Green function elements and derivatives.
Exceptions
std::runtime_errorif input data dimensions mismatch or invalid mode/op is provided.

Integrand for Green function evaluation, Chew (6.15, 6.16): Chew, W.C., Tong, M.S., Hu, B. (2009). Dyadic Green's Function for Layered Media and Integral Equations. In: Integral Equation Methods for Electromagnetic and Elastic Waves. Synthesis Lectures on Computational Electromagnetics. Springer, Cham. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-01707-0_6.

Definition at line 1031 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ IntraIntegrand()

IntegrandIntra SommerfeldIntegrator::IntraIntegrand ( const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_te,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & rq_tm,
const Data2D & inData2D,
dcmplx kr,
dcmplx kz,
const std::string & mode,
const std::string & op = "" )

Computes the intra-layer Green's function integrand.

This function evaluates the intra-layer integrand for a given wavenumber, using radial and axial coordinates. It computes Green function elements, derivatives, and surface integrals based on mode selection and operations.

Parameters
rq_teQuasistatic TE reflection coefficients.
rq_tmQuasistatic TM reflection coefficients.
inData2DData2D structure containing spatial coordinate information.
krComplex radial wavenumber.
kzComplex axial wavenumber.
modeString specifying the mode of operation ('bessel' or 'hankel').
opString specifying additional operations ('plus', 'minus', or empty for default behavior).
Returns
An IntegrandIntra structure containing computed Green function elements and derivatives.
Exceptions
std::runtime_errorif input data dimensions mismatch or invalid mode/op is provided.

Integrand for Green function evaluation, Chew (6.15, 6.16): Chew, W.C., Tong, M.S., Hu, B. (2009). Dyadic Green's Function for Layered Media and Integral Equations. In: Integral Equation Methods for Electromagnetic and Elastic Waves. Synthesis Lectures on Computational Electromagnetics. Springer, Cham. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-01707-0_6.

◆ Secondary()

std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > > SommerfeldIntegrator::Secondary ( dcmplx krho,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > r,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > z1,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > z2,
const std::string & waves )

Computes secondary wave contributions in a layered medium.

This function calculates secondary wave fields based on given wavenumber, radial and axial coordinates, and the type of waves involved.

Parameters
krhoA complex wavenumber in the radial direction.
rA 3D vector containing the radial coordinate values.
z1A 3D vector containing the first axial coordinate values.
z2A 3D vector containing the second axial coordinate values.
wavesA string specifying the type of waves considered (e.g., TE, TM).
Returns
A 4D vector containing:
  • f: The computed field values.
  • fZ1: The first Z1-derivative of the field.
  • fZ2: The first Z2-derivative of the field.
  • fZZ: The mixed Z1-Z2-derivative of the field.

Definition at line 720 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ selectData2D()

Data2D SommerfeldIntegrator::selectData2D ( const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & r,
const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & z1,
const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & z2,
const std::string & path )

Selects and processes 2D data based on the given input parameters.

This function categorizes and stores 2D data depending on the provided path mode ('semi', 'real', or 'imag'). It organizes radial and axial coordinates into matrix or vector formats as needed.

Parameters
rA 2D vector containing the radial coordinate values.
z1A 2D vector containing the first axial coordinate values.
z2A 2D vector containing the second axial coordinate values (optional).
pathA string specifying the mode of data selection ('semi', 'real', or 'imag').
Returns
A Data2D structure containing processed coordinate values and indexing information.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid path is specified.

Pack 2D r/z data depending on path.

  • "semi": keep matrices; dense traversal by linear index.
  • "real": select (r,z) with z >= ratio*r (or z1+z2 >= ratio*r if provided).
  • "imag": select (r,z) with z < ratio*r (or z1+z2 < ratio*r if provided).

Definition at line 553 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ selectData3D()

Data3D SommerfeldIntegrator::selectData3D ( const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & r,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & z1,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & z2,
const std::string & path )

Selects and processes 3D data based on the given input parameters.

This function categorizes and stores 3D data depending on the provided path mode ('semi', 'real', or 'imag'). It organizes radial and axial coordinates into matrix or vector formats as needed.

Parameters
rA 3D vector containing the radial coordinate values.
z1A 3D vector containing the first axial coordinate values.
z2A 3D vector containing the second axial coordinate values.
pathA string specifying the mode of data selection ('semi', 'real', or 'imag').
Returns
A Data3D structure containing processed coordinate values and indexing information.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid path is specified.

Pack 3D r/z1/z2 data depending on path (semi/real/imag). The real/imag split uses |z1 - z2| vs ratio*r.
